title: Chef and Apple Trees

content.problemStatement:
Chef loves to prepare delicious dishes. This time, Chef has decided to prepare a special dish for you, and needs to gather several apples to do so.

Chef has **N** apple trees in his home garden. Each tree has a certain (non-zero) number of apples on it. In order to create his dish, Chef wants to pluck every apple from every tree.

Chef has an unusual method of collecting apples. In a single minute, he can perform the following task:


Pick any subset of trees such that every tree in the subset has the same number of apples.
From each tree in the subset, pluck any number of apples, as long as the number of apples left on the tree equals the number of apples on a tree not in the subset.
 

If all trees have the same number of apples left, Chef can pluck all of the apples remaining in a single minute.

Chef does not want to keep you waiting, so wants to achieve this task in the minimum possible time. Can you tell him what the minimum time required is?
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains an integer N, the number of apple trees. The second line contains N space‑separated positive integers a1, a2, …, aN – the number of apples on each tree.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line – the minimum number of minutes required for Chef to pluck all apples from all trees.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: all trees already have the same number of apples, so Chef can pluck everything in one minute. Test case 2: The minimum time is max/gcd = 3/1 = 3 minutes.
All values equal, GCD=4, max=4 → 4/4 = 1 minute.

solution.approach: The operation allows Chef to reduce the number of apples on any subset of trees that currently have the same count to any lower value that already appears on some other tree. Effectively, in each minute Chef can make all trees share a common divisor of the current multiset. The optimal strategy is to repeatedly bring every tree down to the greatest common divisor (gcd) of all initial counts. Once all trees have the same value (the gcd), Chef can remove the remaining apples in one final minute. Hence the answer equals max(ai) / gcd(ai).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (computing gcd and maximum in a single pass).
